Marathon Petroleum Company LP
YTD Consumed: $767K
YTD Budget: $805K
YTD ACV: $1.0M
Projected Consumed: $1.8M
Projected Budget: $2.0M
Projected ACV: $2.4M
Industry Insights
Marathon Petroleum Company LP stands as the sole customer in this Oil, gas, and energy cohort and demonstrates a systemic architectural anti-pattern critical to refining operations: transactional tiers are overconsumed (Ariba Buying, Database Cloud at 15.8x budget, Joule Studio at 904% over ceiling) while measurement and governance tiers (Process Intelligence, Sourcing, Catalog, BTP across all three domains) remain dormant, structurally preventing the closed-loop visibility that downstream refiners require for margin optimization, turnaround planning, and regulatory compliance.
Marathon has contracted BTP capacity three separate times across Technology Foundation, Data & AI BDC Public, and Finance & Spend Cloud Platform EA yet activated none of it, meaning the foundational integration and extension substrate that every modern refinery digital twin, predictive maintenance scenario, and AI-driven yield optimization initiative requires is entirely homeless—a critical gap for an industry where real-time process data integration directly impacts barrel economics.
Marathon's S/4HANA Private Cloud core is accumulating technical debt at an accelerating rate because Preferred Success consumption has collapsed to 38% YTD, Integration Suite headroom is capped at $7.7K monthly, and BTP extensibility is dormant, forcing every customization, AI agent, and process extension into the ERP core rather than the platform—directly compounding upgrade risk in an industry where ERP stability underpins refinery scheduling, crude slate optimization, and regulatory reporting.
Marathon is running production-scale AI and data workloads (Joule Studio at $190K against $28.8K budget, Database Cloud at $118K against $9.2K budget) on commercial envelopes scoped for pilots while $34K of Preferred Success advisory and ~$250K of Process Intelligence ACV sit unconsumed, indicating a procurement-to-architecture mismatch that exposes the refining business to both commercial true-up risk and ungoverned AI expansion without the process mining foundation refiners need for continuous improvement.
Convene a BTP activation council for Marathon that consolidates all three dormant BTP entitlements into a single governed platform plan with named workloads mapped to refinery-critical scenarios—process data integration, predictive maintenance, and AI-driven yield optimization—to establish the architectural substrate the downstream business requires before further AI or data investments proceed.
Commission an enterprise contract right-sizing engagement for Marathon spanning Database Cloud, Joule Studio, Ariba, Process Intelligence, and Preferred Success to reconcile pilot-scoped ceilings carrying production loads against fully-contracted dormant services, restoring commercial visibility and architectural alignment before the next renewal cycle.
Execute an S/4HANA Private Cloud clean-core architecture review for Marathon that anchors Preferred Success advisory reactivation, Integration Suite capacity planning, and BTP workload routing to the upcoming release roadmap, ensuring extensions and AI agents land on the platform rather than the ERP core ahead of the next upgrade window.
Establish a closed-loop governance program for Marathon spanning Signavio (model-to-mine), Ariba (source-to-pay), and Data & AI (data-to-insight) that mandates activation of measurement and governance tiers as a prerequisite for further transactional tier investment, directly addressing the systemic pattern preventing margin visibility and compliance outcomes critical to refining operations.

Industry Insights
VS Service Company, LLC represents the entire Professional Services cohort and exhibits a split-maturity architecture: operationalized SAP outputs (BDC data fabric at 176%, Joule Studio at 133%, Ariba Buying at 89.5%) are running significantly ahead of contract while the enabling BTP extension and platform layers sit at or near $0 activation — a pattern that in professional services firms creates technical debt in the core, blocks the clean-core extensibility required for client-facing service automation, and undermines the AI-driven decisioning investments already made.
Integration Suite is simultaneously over-consumed in Technology Foundation (133% of budget) and architecturally absent in Finance, meaning the backbone that should enable procurement-to-analytics traceability for utilization tracking, project costing, and margin optimization — critical metrics for professional services profitability — is throttling under load in one domain while leaving Finance workflows disconnected.
Ariba Sourcing at only 42% attainment combined with dormant BDC Public capacity (~$49K unconsumed) reveals a broken closed-loop procurement intelligence chain: negotiated-spend signals from strategic sourcing never reach the data layer, leaving the firm with transactional throughput but no AI-driven category intelligence or savings analytics — a significant gap for a professional services firm where indirect spend optimization directly impacts partner margins.
The portfolio is misallocated rather than under-invested: ~$49K of BDC Public, the full Finance EA commit, and Technology Foundation Other allocations remain dormant while Joule, Integration Suite, and BDC are collectively trending tens of thousands over budget — a rebalance could fund production growth without net new spend and bring consumption closer to the $711K budget ceiling.
Convene an enterprise-wide integration architecture review positioning Integration Suite as the unified backbone for Ariba-to-S/4, S/4-to-BDC, and Joule-to-LoB flows, right-sizing capacity to aggregate demand across Finance, Data & AI, and Technology Foundation rather than siloed domain budgets.
Launch a BTP activation workshop targeting dormant BDC Public, Technology Foundation Other, and Finance EA entitlements to identify first extension workloads — agentic sourcing, Ariba-to-BDC spend analytics, and Joule-embedded process extensions — that convert stranded capacity into clean-core operational outcomes essential for scalable service delivery.
Initiate a consolidated true-up and portfolio reallocation across BDC, Joule Studio, Integration Suite, and Database Cloud, mapping each overage to a documented business outcome and redirecting dormant EA capacity to fund the rebalance without incremental ACV.
Establish a single enterprise consumption governance cadence spanning Data & AI, Technology Foundation, and Finance to manage the SAP estate as one architectural portfolio, eliminating the current pattern of isolated overages alongside stranded contracted capacity that erodes both budget efficiency and architectural coherence.

Target Corporation
YTD Consumed: $2.6M
YTD Budget: $2.4M
YTD ACV: $3.1M
Projected Consumed: $6.2M
Projected Budget: $5.8M
Projected ACV: $7.5M
Industry Insights
Target Corporation stands alone in this single-customer retail cohort, but its architecture represents a cautionary pattern for the industry: transactional spend management layers (Ariba P2P at 121%, Concur at 106-130%) are scaling while the data backbone (BDC at $0), planning capability (SAC Planning plateau), and AI surface (Joule/Build at 15%) required to convert that transaction volume into retail-differentiating outcomes like demand sensing, inventory optimization, and supplier collaboration remain completely dormant.
Target's Integration Suite is the sole active platform fabric in its estate, yet without BDC activation, CPEA governance, or a consuming extension layer, the retailer is moving data point-to-point across P2P and T&E without the API management, event mesh, or closed-loop architecture needed to feed real-time signals into planning and decisioning — a critical gap in an industry where supply chain agility and margin management depend on unified data flow.
Target's Preferred Success entitlements across Ariba, Concur, and SCM are reinforcing consumption inertia rather than architectural progression — success hours are drawing down against domains that are either over-burning (P2P/T&E) or architecturally hollow (SCM shows no visible execution platform), meaning guided value is not steering the retailer toward the unified data, planning, and AI architecture the contract was designed to deliver.
Target's contracted architecture commits to a unified data-and-AI retail operating model, but the YTD picture — $575K Ariba P2P overage accumulating while $27.8K BDC, $83K Integration Suite CPEA, and $92K SAC Planning sit idle alongside dormant Build/Joule capacity — signals the retailer will arrive at renewal having paid for an AI-ready foundation while operating a transactional-only estate, undermining both investment ROI and negotiating position.
Convene an enterprise architecture review with Target's CIO, CFO, and Supply Chain leadership to re-sequence BTP foundation activation (BDC, Integration Suite CPEA governance, Build/Joule) as the prerequisite to converting transactional scale into differentiated retail outcomes like demand-driven replenishment and margin optimization.
Consolidate Target's three Preferred Success engagements into a single outcome-based program with success hours explicitly tied to activating dormant architecture — BDC data products for merchandising analytics, SAC Planning use cases for demand forecasting, and Multi Bank Connect for treasury visibility — rather than flat drawdown against over-consuming domains.
Open a unified commercial conversation with Target that pairs the ~$575K Ariba P2P and Concur ceiling true-ups with reallocation of idle BDC, Integration Suite CPEA, Build, Joule, and SAC Planning entitlement, presenting one enterprise architecture commitment that positions renewal around strategic value rather than disconnected line-item negotiations.
Execute a Supply Chain architecture discovery to determine whether the absence of IBP, EWM, TM, and Ariba SCM execution products is a reporting gap or genuine void, then link findings to an integrated retail planning roadmap connecting SCM to SAC Planning and BDC to unlock the supply chain resilience and inventory optimization capabilities critical for retail competitiveness.

Industry Insights
Border States Industries stands alone in this single-customer cohort but exhibits the defining wholesale distribution architectural paradox: BTP consumption at 103% of budget masks severe LoB under-activation, with Commerce Cloud at ~32% attainment and HXM suites (Onboarding 48%, Career Development 42%) leaving over $130K of experience-layer capacity dormant — directly undermining the digital channel growth and workforce productivity that drive distributor margin in a low-margin, high-velocity industry.
Border States has achieved rare platform-layer velocity with Joule Studio at 208% and Data & AI at 113.7% attainment, positioning them as a potential AI capability leader among wholesale distributors, yet this investment is architecturally disconnected from the talent, commerce, and finance domains where AI-driven automation should deliver measurable distributor outcomes like order-to-cash acceleration and warehouse labor optimization.
Border States demonstrates a repeating enterprise anti-pattern critical to wholesale distribution operations: architecturally complete process suites in HXM (Performance→Compensation→Career Development) and Finance (ExpenseIt→Expense→Audit→Pay→Reporting) are contracted end-to-end but operated as disconnected modules — Compensation at $0 and Concur Expense collapsing to $507 — blocking the closed-loop spend governance and workforce cost control that protect distributor margins.
Commerce Cloud's ~$90K dormant capacity represents a strategic miss for a wholesale distributor where B2B digital channel penetration directly correlates to customer retention and order frequency; Border States is investing in AI agents via Joule that have no activated commerce front-end to serve, inverting the typical distributor digital transformation sequence.
Conduct a BTP CPEA FinOps decomposition for Border States that attributes consumption by service (HANA Cloud, Datasphere, AI Core, Joule Studio, Integration Suite) to sponsoring LoB use cases, converting the current undifferentiated platform spend into a value-attribution model before the $134K remaining Data & AI budget and Joule overrun force reactive commercial conversations.
Execute an integration coherence diagnostic across Border States' broken process chains — Performance→Compensation, Recruiting→Onboarding, Concur Expense→Pay/Audit/Reporting — to determine whether failures are configuration, data pipeline, or adoption issues, since repairing these chains unlocks the distributor-critical outcomes of labor cost visibility and spend compliance.
Design a Commerce Cloud activation roadmap for Border States that surrounds the dormant ~$90K capacity with Joule-driven buyer experiences and CX ecosystem products, converting platform-layer AI investment into a B2B digital channel growth initiative aligned to wholesale distribution's competitive imperative of customer self-service and order automation.
Establish an enterprise architecture governance forum with Border States that replaces module-by-module consumption tracking with a portfolio view tying the ~$700K+ FY26 SAP footprint to distributor-specific KPIs — digital order penetration, warehouse labor productivity, and spend-under-management — to surface where architectural completeness is failing to deliver business outcomes.
